mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in real retail sales proxy demand improves near-term revenue expectations and investor risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available RSAFS vintage before 2020-01-15 was 2019-11-01 value 527994.0
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce a positive Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns in at least 60% of walk-forward annual windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate decline crossing below a 25 basis point 3-month drop signals active monetary easing, lowers discount rates, and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change crosses below -0.25 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS vintage before 2020-01-15 was 2019-12-01 value 1.55
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional base rate or mean triggered returns are not above non-triggered returns at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment exceeding 0.3 percentage points over 3 months marks deteriorating labor income and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change crosses above +0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE vintage before 2020-01-15 was 2019-12-01 value 3.5
failure_condition: Reject if after this trigger the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases and the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate is flat or rising, markets price tighter real financial conditions and lower equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation acceleration > 0 and FEDFUNDS 3-month change >= 0
evidence_refs: fact:latest available CPIAUCSL vintage before 2020-01-15 was 2019-12-01 value 258.501
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL acceleration and non-falling FEDFUNDS condition has non-negative average 60-day excess returns relative to unconditional returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth crossing below zero indicates weakening manufacturing output and earnings momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available INDPRO vintage before 2020-01-15 was 2019-11-01 value 109.677
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO negative-growth triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 65% of cases or show a non-negative Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising above their 12-month average while the federal funds rate is below its 12-month average signals rate-sensitive demand recovery, supporting cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its 12-month moving average and FEDFUNDS is below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:latest available HOUST vintage before 2020-01-15 was 2019-11-01 value 1365.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-starts recovery and low-rate condition fails to deliver average 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional average in at least two consecutive walk-forward test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth acceleration combined with unemployment below 4 percent creates late-cycle wage-pressure risk and reduces valuation support, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available PAYEMS vintage before 2020-01-15 was 2019-12-01 value 152383.0
fact:latest available UNRATE vintage before 2020-01-15 was 2019-12-01 value 3.5
failure_condition: Reject if this late-cycle labor condition is followed by average 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional average or if the interaction term has the wrong sign in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase crossing below its trailing 6-month average signals near-term disinflation, reduces rate-hike risk, and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 1-month change crosses below its trailing 6-month average change
evidence_refs: fact:latest available CPIAUCSL vintage before 2020-01-15 was 2019-12-01 value 258.501
failure_condition: Reject if CPI disinflation triggers do not produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than the unconditional base rate or the 5-day event-study average is negative.
